tool.name: ParcelExporter
tool.summary: Bypasses ArcGIS Online export restrictions by connecting directly to the GeoOhio Statewide Parcel REST service. No map layers need to be loaded into your project. Supports targeted extraction by Statewide, ODOT District, MPO Area, County, or a Custom Boundary shapefile or feature class. All geometry and attribute data are preserved in a local File Geodatabase.
tool.usage: 1. Open the tool from the Catalog pane (no layers needed in your map). 2. Choose a Filter Area from the dropdown. 3. Fill in the sub-parameter that appears for your chosen filter. 4. Select an Output Folder. A GDB named Ohio_Regional_Export.gdb will be created there. 5. Click Run.

param.name: filter_type
param.dialogexplanation: Choose the geographic scale for your extraction. "Statewide" downloads all Ohio parcel records (slow — millions of features). "ODOT District", "MPO Area", and "County" perform server-side filtering so only the records you need are transferred, making them much faster. "Specific Boundary" lets you use any polygon layer as a spatial clip boundary.

param.name: dist_num
param.dialogexplanation: Select one of Ohio's 12 ODOT Districts. The tool contains a built-in county mapping for each district so no manual lookup is required. District 1 = NW Ohio | District 2 = NW (Toledo area) | District 3 = N Central | District 4 = NE Ohio (Stark, Summit, Mahoning) | District 5 = E Central | District 6 = Central (Columbus/Franklin) | District 7 = W Central (Dayton area) | District 8 = SW (Cincinnati) | District 9 = S Central | District 10 = SE Ohio | District 11 = E Ohio | District 12 = Greater Cleveland

param.name: mpo_name
param.dialogexplanation: Select a Metropolitan Planning Organization from Ohio's 17 designated MPOs. The tool maps each MPO to its member counties and downloads only those parcels. AMATS = Akron | NOACA = Cleveland | MORPC = Columbus | OKI = Cincinnati | MVRPC = Dayton | TMACOG = Toledo | SCATS = Canton | Eastgate = Youngstown | LACRPC = Lima | LCATS = Newark | RCRPC = Mansfield | ERPC = Sandusky | CCSTCC = Springfield | BHJ = Steubenville | KYOVA = Huntington | BOMTS = Wheeling area | WWW = Parkersburg area

param.name: county_name
param.dialogexplanation: Select any of Ohio's 88 counties from the dropdown list. County-level extraction is the fastest single-area download option. Supported counties: Adams, Allen, Ashland, Ashtabula, Athens, Auglaize, Belmont, Brown, Butler, Carroll, Champaign, Clark, Clermont, Clinton, Columbiana, Coshocton, Crawford, Cuyahoga, Darke, Defiance, Delaware, Erie, Fairfield, Fayette, Franklin, Fulton, Gallia, Geauga, Greene, Guernsey, Hamilton, Hancock, Hardin, Harrison, Henry, Highland, Hocking, Holmes, Huron, Jackson, Jefferson, Knox, Lake, Lawrence, Licking, Logan, Lorain, Lucas, Madison, Mahoning, Marion, Medina, Meigs, Mercer, Miami, Monroe, Montgomery, Morgan, Morrow, Muskingum, Noble, Ottawa, Paulding, Perry, Pickaway, Pike, Portage, Preble, Putnam, Richland, Ross, Sandusky, Scioto, Seneca, Shelby, Stark, Summit, Trumbull, Tuscarawas, Union, Van Wert, Vinton, Warren, Washington, Wayne, Williams, Wood, Wyandot.

param.name: boundary_feat
param.dialogexplanation: Browse to a polygon layer (shapefile or feature class) that defines your study area — for example a township boundary, a buffer zone, or a custom planning area. The tool will spatially intersect the statewide parcel service against this boundary and export only the records that fall within or touch it. The boundary layer must be visible in your current ArcGIS Pro project.

param.name: out_folder
param.dialogexplanation: Select the destination folder for your results. A File Geodatabase named "Ohio_Regional_Export.gdb" will be created here automatically. The exported feature class will be named Parcels_{FilterType} inside that GDB. Using a local drive (C: or D:) rather than a network path will give the fastest write speeds for large datasets.
